news.headline: Rocket Lab chosen by NASA for Neutron launch services under VADR contract, expanding access to space. #RKLB #NASA
news.summary: Rocket Lab USA, Inc. based in Long Beach, California, recently disclosed that they have come to a mutual agreement with NASA to provide Neutron launch services through Rocket Lab’s current VADR contract. This agreement involves Rocket Lab’s medium-lift reusable rocket Neutron. The company, listed on Nasdaq as RKLB, is a prominent figure in the field of launch services and space systems. By including Neutron in the services offered under the VADR contract, Rocket Lab aims to enhance accessibility to space, showcasing its commitment towards expanding opportunities in the sector.

news.headline: Elastic (NYSE: ESTC) to present at 27th Annual Needham Growth Conference on Jan 16, 2025, at 12:00 p.m. PT / 3:00 p.m. ET.
news.summary: A company known as Elastic (NYSE: ESTC), specializing in Search AI, is set to have its management team participate in the 27th Annual Needham Growth Conference on Thursday, January 16, 2025, at 12:00 p.m. PT / 3:00 p.m. ET. Interested individuals can access a live webcast and replay of the conference on Elastic’s Investor Relations page at ir.elastic.co. Elastic, identified by the ticker symbol ESTC on the New York Stock Exchange, focuses on empowering users to access real-time answers from all their data on a large scale, serving as the Search AI Company.

news.headline: Myriad Genetics partners with sports broadcaster Hannah Storm to promote awareness about family cancer history.
news.summary: Myriad Genetics has entered into a partnership with sports broadcaster Hannah Storm to raise awareness about the significance of understanding one's family cancer history. The agreement aims to emphasize the importance of knowing and discussing pertinent information within families regarding cancer. This collaboration seeks to educate individuals on the impact of familial cancer history on personal health.

news.headline: Alpine Banks of Colorado declares $0.21/share dividend for Class B nonvoting common shares on Jan 27, 2025. Class A dividend basis: 1/150th.
news.summary: Alpine Banks of Colorado, located in Glenwood Springs, recently disclosed details about their upcoming quarterly cash dividend distribution. Shareholders holding Class B nonvoting common shares will receive $0.21 per share on January 27, 2025. The record date for this dividend is January 20, 2025. According to the company's Articles of Incorporation, dividends for Class B nonvoting common stock are determined by the Board of Directors, set at one-one-hundred-fiftieth (1/150th) of the amount per share declared for each Class A voting common stock share. These Class B nonvoting common shares can be traded publicly on the OTCQX® Best Market under the stock symbol “ALPIB.”

news.headline: Betting markets expect a strong increase in December nonfarm payrolls, contrary to Goldman Sachs' forecast of 125,000 jobs. Traders betting on numbers above 150,000 have a 70% chance of winning, with odds suggesting 187,000 new jobs in December 2024. Bureau of Labor Statistics to release official report Friday at 8:30 a.m. ET.
news.summary: Betting markets are indicating a significant discrepancy in expectations for December's nonfarm payrolls between traders and Wall Street economists. While economists are predicting a modest increase of 125,000 jobs, betting platforms suggest a more positive outlook with implied odds pointing towards a growth of 187,000 jobs. Traders foresee a 70% chance of the job numbers surpassing 150,000, and a 40% likelihood of it exceeding 200,000. The official jobs report by the Bureau of Labor Statistics is set to be released on Friday at 8:30 a.m. ET, providing clarity on the actual figures. This optimistic sentiment in the betting markets reflects a potential upturn in the labor market compared to the more conservative estimates by economists.

news.headline: Canadian PM Trudeau: If trump does impose 25% tariffs on imports from Canada, everything the American consumers buy from canada is suddenly going to get a lot more expensive.

news.headline: Belva AI launches Belva Engineer, AI-powered platform to enhance software development. Includes blueprint-driven features.
news.summary: In Bellevue, Washington, on January 9, 2025, Belva AI introduced Belva Engineer, an innovative AI-driven development platform. This platform aims to enhance software development by combining AI automation with unique features such as blueprint-driven development. The unveiling of Belva Engineer represents a significant step forward in the tech industry, promising increased efficiency and productivity for developers. This unveiling marks a milestone in the industry's evolution towards utilizing cutting-edge technology for more streamlined development processes.

news.headline: eNavvi offers unlimited prescription credits to California physicians amidst wildfires.aimed at reducing healthcare costs.
news.summary: Today, eNavvi declared its commitment to supporting California during the ongoing fires by offering unlimited prescription credits to physicians statewide. This move comes in response to the significant impact of the fires on Los Angeles and neighboring regions. The company's initiative seeks to alleviate the strain on healthcare professionals as they address the crisis.

news.headline: SynCardia Systems receives first patent in China for Emperor Next-Generation Total Artificial Heart.
news.summary: A company known as SynCardia Systems, LLC, a subsidiary of Picard Medical, Inc., based in Tucson, Arizona, revealed on January 9, 2025, that the China National Intellectual Property Administration (CNIPA) has granted them a significant patent related to total artificial heart technology. SynCardia is recognized as the global frontrunner in this field. This development marks a crucial advancement for the company, demonstrating its commitment to innovation and securing its position in the market. Such recognition from CNIPA highlights the importance of this patent for SynCardia, potentially leading to further advancements in artificial heart technology.

news.headline: Holly secures $2.2M funding to modernize government hiring, aiming to fill million-job workforce vacancies faster with AI platform.
news.summary: In New York, an AI-powered platform named Holly that aims to modernize government hiring revealed the successful completion of its pre-seed funding round. The round raised $2.2 million, exceeding the initial target, and was spearheaded by J2 Ventures. Other contributors included Roble Ventures, Gaingels, as well as notable angel investors. The funds obtained will bolster Holly's platform in addressing the pressing issue of a million-job government workforce vacancy crisis. Through its innovative approach, Holly is dedicated to revamping antiquated government hiring procedures to streamline the recruitment process for a whopping 24 million public sector positions.

news.headline: Crunch Fitness in Los Angeles opens doors for wildfire-affected community to charge phones, use wifi, and take showers.
news.summary: Crunch Fitness in Los Angeles has opened its doors to the community amid ongoing wildfires, offering a space for individuals to charge their phones, utilize wifi, and access shower facilities. This generous initiative aims to support those affected by the wildfires while providing essential services. As of January 9, 2025, the gym is welcoming individuals in need of such amenities as they navigate the challenges posed by the natural disaster.

news.headline: Gatos Silver announced 2024 production results at their CLG mine in Mexico, with silver production meeting top guidance end.
news.summary: On January 9, 2025, Gatos Silver, Inc. shared production figures for the quarter and year concluding on December 31, 2024, from the Cerro Los Gatos ("CLG") mine in Mexico, a venture the company holds a 70% stake in. Their silver production for 2024 was within the high end of the projected range, and silver equivalent production slightly surpassed the upper limit of the anticipated range. The guidance for both these metrics was adjusted upwards in October 2024.

news.headline: TriNetX's CEO Gadi Lachman to resign after 11 years, will remain on board. Largest global data source. Jan 9, 2025.
news.summary: Gadi Lachman, the Founder, President, and CEO of TriNetX, will be ending his 11-year operating role but will continue serving on the company's board. The announcement was made in Cambridge, Massachusetts on January 9, 2025. TriNetX is known as the largest global provider of real-world data.
